pages.text: MultiLib ships with a small set of demo blocks so you can see multiblock structures working in-game without writing any code of your own. This entry introduces each one.

pages.title: Example Multiblock Part
pages.text: A plain structural block used to fill out the body of the $(l:example_multiblock)$(bold)Example Multiblock$(). It has no special behavior on its own - it's just an ingredient the pattern matcher looks for.

pages.title: Example Multiblock Controller
pages.text: The $(bold)core$() block of the Example Multiblock. Placing this block once the rest of the pattern is already assembled triggers formation automatically - see $(l:example_multiblock)$(bold)Example Multiblock$() for the full pattern.

pages.title: Example Directional Multiblock Controller
pages.text: A variant core block that remembers the direction it was placed in, like a furnace. Its multiblock pattern is asymmetric, so the ghost preview always shows the structure facing the way the core is actually pointed, instead of rotating to face the player.

pages.title: Multiblock Wrench
pages.text: A tool item for manually triggering multiblock formation on structures that support wrench activation, instead of relying purely on automatic detection. Right-click the core block of an assembled pattern with the wrench to attempt formation.
